description_long: This is a small, rustic wooden stool with a thick, semi-circular seat and three sturdy cylindrical legs. The seat surface is flat and features visible circular join marks, suggesting traditional joinery techniques. The wood has a natural, unfinished appearance with a matte texture and visible grain patterns, giving it a handcrafted look. The legs are slightly splayed for stability and are attached directly to the underside of the seat. There are no decorative elements, backrest, or footrest, emphasizing its utilitarian design. The stool is compact and robust, suitable for use in parks, public spaces, or as casual seating in urban environments.
description: A rustic, semi-circular wooden stool with three cylindrical legs and a flat, matte-finished seat. The design is simple, sturdy, and utilitarian, with visible wood grain and join marks.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat, semi-circular seat and all three cylindrical legs, with the central leg positioned at the back.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the seat and the splayed arrangement of the legs, with two legs visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the straight edge of the seat and the central rear leg, with the other two legs angled outward.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the seat's thickness and the angled placement of the legs, similar to view 1 but from the other side.
